concepts.finding:
Our primary contributions can be summarized as follows:
• We propose a novel model called Contrastive Pre-training for
Sequential Recommendation (CP4Rec) which infers accurate
user representations with only users’ interaction behaviors.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
We propose a novel model called Contrastive Pre-
training for Sequential Recommendation (CP4Rec), which utilizes
the contrastive pre-training framework to extract meaningful user
patterns and further encode the user representation effectively.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
5
CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK
In this paper, we propose a novel model called Contrastive Pre-
training for Sequential Recommendation (CP4Rec), which can learn
the effective user representation model only with item interaction
information.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Exten-
sive experiment results show that our CP4Rec achieves significant
improvements and outperforms the start-of-the-art baselines, espe-
cially when limited training data is available.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Comprehensive experiments on four
public datasets demonstrate that CP4Rec achieves state-of-the-art
performance over existing baselines especially when limited train-
ing data is available.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

with this problem, various methods have been proposed to make
sequential recommendations by capturing users’dynamic interests

is how to infer an accurate representation for each user through
their historical interactions.
